purpose: Tie the completed P12 governance batch to final convergence gates, commit checkpoints, ignored runtime paths, aiflow migration limitations, known risks, and release-blocking verification.

aiflowExecution.blocker: aiflow CLI status is currently unavailable because the adjacent /Users/bytedance/Codes/go/src/github/aiflow repository is mid-migration and fails to compile runtime trajectory types.
aiflowExecution.goflyImpact: P12 tasks were submitted through root aiflow.yaml and completed with durable docs/reference evidence plus per-task commits. Runtime .aiflow state remains ignored and commit and push are owned by the current agent or human.

previousBatchHandoff.reason: P11 convergence was carried forward into the P12 active batch after RPC benchmark budget promotion review, generated real branch replay contract, and hosted cloud-native live CI closure completed with per-task commits.
previousBatchHandoff.completionPolicy: complete GOFLY-P12-CONVERGENCE-EVIDENCE by updating governance-boundary-inventory, governance-dashboard-contract, and governance-convergence-verification, then run make governance-boundary-inventory-check and make governance-report-check, with commits owned by the current agent or human

knownRisks.status: P11 completion uses durable gofly evidence because adjacent aiflow runtime types currently fail to compile during its own migration.
knownRisks.followUp: After aiflow compiles again, run aiflow status against the gofly root config and reconcile ignored runtime status with the durable P11 roadmap evidence.

knownRisks.status: P12 RPC budget promotion review is complete, but Tier 1 promotion remains held behind release-train evidence and blocking RPC benchmark budget gates.
knownRisks.followUp: Promote RPC surfaces only after hosted release-train evidence, resolver and balancer contracts, streaming compatibility, allocation budgets, and Kitex rollback evidence remain stable.

knownRisks.status: P12 adds a release-blocking hosted cloud-native live render CI job, while Docker-backed reference app and full release artifact scans still depend on hosted tools being available.
knownRisks.followUp: Keep hosted Helm and kube validator evidence blocking, then close the remaining Docker-backed reference app, Trivy, and release evidence artifact loops in later batches.

knownRisks.status: .aiflow, .harness, .tmp-test, .trae, coverage.out, and docs/superpowers remain runtime or local evidence paths and must not be committed.
knownRisks.followUp: Keep runtime evidence ignored and stage only durable governance contracts or source changes.

nextRoundRecommendations.action: Promote only RPC surfaces with stable hosted release-train evidence into allocation-blocking budgets while keeping latency claims report-only until parity data is durable.
Move the reference app from topology contracts to Docker-backed live integration for SQL outbox, cache, stream, discovery, and observability dependencies.
Close hosted release supply-chain artifacts by validating SBOM, checksums, provenance, Docker digest, Trivy output, and release evidence uploads in tag CI.
